def: A language model fine-tuned on your own writing so it reproduces your voice and style.
Feature/Training/Inference — the three-pipeline pattern every ML system reduces to.
Turns raw data into versioned features and writes them to a feature store.
Consumes features to train/fine-tune a model and writes it to a model registry.
Loads the trained model + features to serve predictions, often behind an API.
A central store of versioned, shareable features used for training and inference.
A versioned catalogue of trained models with metadata, the source of truth for deployment.
Practices to automate, version, test, monitor and reproduce ML systems in production.
MLOps specialised for LLMs: prompt monitoring, eval, dataset/model versioning, CT/CI/CD.
Extract-Transform-Load: crawl/standardise raw data and load it into a store.
A central store for collected/processed data; here MongoDB holds the raw crawled text.
Retrieval-Augmented Generation: fetch relevant documents and add them to the prompt to ground answers.
A dense vector capturing the meaning of text, used for semantic search.
A database that indexes embeddings for fast nearest-neighbour (semantic) retrieval.
Splitting documents into smaller passages so they embed and retrieve well.
A second pass that reorders retrieved candidates by relevance before they hit the prompt.
Rewriting/augmenting a query into variations to retrieve a broader candidate set.
Letting the model extract structured filters (metadata) from a natural-language query.
Combining sparse keyword search with dense vector search for better recall.
Continuing training of a pretrained model on task/domain data to specialise it.
Supervised Fine-Tuning: train on instruction→response pairs so a base model follows instructions.
Curated prompt/response pairs used to teach a model to follow instructions.
Low-Rank Adaptation: train small added matrices instead of all weights for cheap fine-tuning.
LoRA on a quantized (4-bit) base model, cutting memory so big models fine-tune on one GPU.
Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning: update only a small subset of parameters (e.g. LoRA).
Storing weights/activations in lower precision (e.g. 4/8-bit) to shrink and speed up models.
A file format for quantized models run by llama.cpp on CPU/consumer hardware.
Tuning a model toward preferred answers using chosen-vs-rejected comparisons.
Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback: align a model using a reward model trained on preferences.
Direct Preference Optimization: align directly on chosen/rejected pairs, no separate reward model.
A model trained to score outputs by human preference, used to guide alignment.
Designing prompts (instructions, examples, context) to steer model outputs.
Putting a few worked examples in the prompt to demonstrate the task.
Prompting the model to reason step by step before answering.
When a model states fluent but false or unsupported information.
The maximum number of tokens a model can attend to at once.
The sub-word unit a model reads/generates; cost and limits are measured in tokens.
The transformer mechanism that weighs how much each token attends to others.
Cached key/value tensors from past tokens so generation doesn't recompute them.
A memory-efficient attention kernel that speeds up training and inference.
Use a small draft model to propose tokens a big model verifies in batches, for speed.
Dynamically packing concurrent requests into GPU batches to raise throughput.
Splitting a model across multiple GPUs (tensor/pipeline) to fit and speed it up.
How many tokens/requests a system serves per unit time.
Time to produce a response (or first token) for a single request.
Production input distribution P(X) shifting away from training data.
The learned input→output relationship P(y|X) becoming outdated over time.
Collecting and visualising system/model metrics to detect degradation.
Explaining a system's internal state to find the root cause of an issue.
Continuous Integration/Delivery: automatically build, test and ship code and models.
Continuous Training: automatically retrain pipelines on new data or detected drift.
A system that schedules and coordinates pipeline steps in the right order.
Directed Acyclic Graph: the dependency graph of pipeline steps.
Any versioned file produced in the ML lifecycle (dataset, model, logs) with metadata.
Logging metrics/params across training runs to compare and pick the best model.
Capturing prompt/response traces to debug and observe an LLM app in production.
An LLM that plans and calls tools/steps to accomplish a goal.
The orchestrator used here; runs pipelines/steps and versions artifacts across stacks.
The NoSQL document database used as the raw-data warehouse.
The vector database used to store embeddings for RAG.
Hub used here as the model registry and source of open models/datasets.
The experiment tracker used to log training/eval metrics.
The open-source prompt-monitoring tool (by Comet) used for LLM traces.
AWS managed ML platform used to train on GPUs and deploy the model as an API.
AWS serverless API for pretrained foundation models (contrasted with SageMaker).
Containers used to run the whole stack locally and ship reproducible builds.
The Python web framework used to expose the inference REST API.
Python data-validation library used to enforce typed schemas.
A library that speeds up and shrinks memory for LLM fine-tuning.
A high-throughput LLM serving engine (PagedAttention, continuous batching).
A framework for composing LLM calls, retrieval and tools into chains.
Code that scrapes posts/articles/repos from the web into the data warehouse.
Minimum Viable Product: the smallest valuable version that maximises value over effort.
